markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement Handshake-managed Tree-sitter parser adapter with language detection and parser version receipts. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: TreeSitterParserAdapter leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TreeSitterParserAdapter is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: No input-size or node-count guard before tree-sitter parse (parser.rs:235 parse). A multi-megabyte or pathologically deep file is parsed unconditionally; tree-sitter will not panic (offsets are bounded), but a directory run over an adversarial/giant file consumes unbounded time+memory. The module docstring promises 'bounded' operation but the parser enforces no bound.

node_field_name (parser.rs:333) re-walks the parent with a fresh cursor for EVERY node via Node::id() linear scan; combined with the full-tree flatten this is O(nodes * siblings) per file. Cheap for normal files, but a wide flat file (thousands of siblings under one parent, e.g. a generated array literal) is quadratic.
reorder_preorder (parser.rs:355) clones every AstNode into a second Vec; the whole named-node stream is materialized twice. Memory ~2x the node count with no cap.
evasion_probes: Verified parse() fail-closed paths: set_language err -> typed CodeParseError (no panic); parser.parse returns Option, handled by ok_or_else (parser.rs:245); a tree WITH syntax errors returns root_has_error=true rather than erroring -> correct per MT-108 contract.

Verified node_text (parser.rs:166) uses source.get(range) returning Option, so a non-UTF-8-boundary byte range yields None, never a slice panic. parse() takes &str so the input is already valid UTF-8 (Rust invariant); invalid-UTF-8 FILES are rejected upstream at read_to_string, see MT-108 note.
Confirmed byte_range_to_line_range uses saturating_sub/max guards (parser.rs:427) so empty/zero-length ranges do not underflow.
Confirmed language detection ordering handles compound suffixes (.d.ts before .ts, .tsx before .ts) correctly (parser.rs:91).

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened cf1b690d: Tree-sitter FFI parse wrapped in catch_unwind; raw-bytes/UTF-8 read-failure handling.

evidence.finding: Unbounded tree-sitter parse: no input-size or node-count guard (V1 concern; catch_unwind only)
evidence.fix: MAX_PARSE_SOURCE_BYTES 2MiB enforced before parse + MAX_PARSE_NODES 400k bounding the walk; typed CodeParseError degrades to engine repair-queue skip; parse_with_caps for tighter budgets
evidence.tests: lib knowledge_code_index::parser 7 passed incl. oversized_source_is_rejected_before_parse + node_ceiling_degrades_to_typed_failure

findings.title: V1 unbounded-parse concern NOT closed: no input-size or node-count guard before tree-sitter parse
findings.evidence: parser.rs:246-263 wraps parse in catch_unwind (the panic vector the hardening commit cf1b690d addressed), but there is still NO byte/node cap before parse, and engine.rs:258 calls adapter.parse(text) with no pre-parse file-size cap (grep for MAX_FILE/metadata/len> in engine.rs finds none). A multi-MB or pathologically deep adversarial file is parsed unconditionally; catch_unwind prevents a panic but not unbounded time/memory. The module still promises bounded operation.
findings.fix: Add a byte cap (skip+repair-queue oversized files) and/or a node-count ceiling in parse_inner, mirroring the SCIP caps (scip.rs:152), before invoking tree-sitter.

missing_tests: test: an oversized / pathologically deep file is skipped (or capped) rather than parsed unbounded
adversarial_review_v2.note: VERIFIED FIXED from V1: FFI panic now caught (catch_unwind, parser.rs:249) and raw-bytes/UTF-8 read failures handled. Residual: the size/node guard the V1 reviewer flagged remains absent.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
